LAW AND MOTION 302, CROSS DEFENDANT JP BUILDERS, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT OF LISA LEEBOVE AND STEPHEN STEINBERG. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: OVERRULED BASED ON LACK OF JURISDICTION. THE 2ND CROSS-COMPLAINT IS PROPERLY CHARACTERIZED AS AN AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT THAT SUPERSEDED THE ORIGINAL CROSS-COMPLAINT. THE SUBSEQUENT DISMISSAL OF THE ORIGINAL CROSS-COMPLAINT WAS A NULLITY. OVERRULED AS TO 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 3RD AND 4TH CAUSES OF ACTION. LEEBOVE TO PLEAD TERMS OF THE IMPLIED CONTRACT WITH MORE PARTICULARITY. THE COURT NOTES THAT LEEBOVE RELIES ON THE EXPRESS CONTRACT TO ARGUE THAT THE LIEN IS EXCESSIVE. OVERRULED AS TO 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR UNJUST ENRICHMENT. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION. CIVIL CODE SECTION 1584.5 CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS AS A MATTER OF LAW BECAUSE THE PARTIES AGREE THAT THEY WERE SUBJECT TO SOME SORT OF CONTRACTUAL RELATIONSHIP. ORDER SIGNED.
